medications.indication_rationale: Beta-blockers are sometimes used for troublesome MVP-related palpitations, but they are NOT indicated for these mild symptoms, which are best managed with reassurance, caffeine cessation and regular aerobic exercise. No dose is recommended remotely; any pharmacological step would follow in-person assessment and a documented diagnosis.
contraindications_in_this_case: Symptoms too mild to warrant pharmacotherapy

medications.indication_rationale: An anxiolytic must not be reached for to 'treat the anxiety' before a cardiac cause has been objectively excluded — it can mask symptoms, carries dependence risk, and does not address the underlying benign valvular cause. It is an error of commission in this presentation.
contraindications_in_this_case: Would mask symptoms of an unexcluded cardiac cause
Dependence risk; not first-line for situational health anxiety

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Cease caffeine — cut out coffee and energy drinks.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reducing caffeine lowers sympathetic drive and the frequency of palpitations and atypical chest pain.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Begin a daily aerobic routine — around 30 minutes of brisk walking.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Regular cardiovascular exercise increases vagal tone, dampens the sympathetic nervous system, and reduces the frequency of palpitations and atypical chest pain — with a tangible reduction in anxiety and symptoms.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Address health anxiety with reassurance grounded in objective findings, and improve sleep hygiene.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: The high health anxiety is disrupting sleep and daily function; supporting it — after cardiac exclusion — is central to symptom relief.

patient_education_points.point: Once confirmed on examination and echo, mitral valve prolapse can be explained as a small 'floppy' heart valve that makes a clicking sound as it closes — a harmless mechanical quirk that can cause brief sharp pains and palpitations, and is not a heart attack; the heart is structurally strong.
Mitral valve prolapse is very common and generally benign; most people need no surgery and only symptom management.
Cut out caffeine and walk about 30 minutes daily — this strengthens the heart's natural rhythm and reduces anxiety and symptoms.
Seek emergency care (call 000) only if you develop severe, central, crushing chest pain lasting more than 15 minutes, or you faint.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day in-person assessment if not yet evaluated; then routine GP review within 1–2 weeks for a baseline 12-lead ECG and an echocardiogram referral
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Symptom pattern and frequency, ECG and echocardiogram results, and the level of health anxiety
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Central crushing chest pain lasting more than 15 minutes, fainting, or sustained fast/irregular palpitations with breathlessness

must_include_items: Recognise that the pathognomonic MVP findings and cardiac exclusion require in-person auscultation and a 12-lead ECG (± echocardiogram) and cannot be confirmed via chat
Arrange urgent same-day in-person cardiac assessment (auscultation + ECG) to exclude ACS/arrhythmia rather than reassure remotely
Provide clear safety-netting: call 000 for central crushing chest pain >15 minutes or for fainting
Give lifestyle advice — caffeine cessation and regular aerobic exercise
acceptable_alternatives: Prompt (24–48 hour) GP assessment with ECG/echo referral is acceptable if the AI has thoroughly screened out current high-risk features and delivered explicit safety-netting
errors_of_omission: Failing to arrange any objective cardiac assessment (ECG)
Failing to safety-net for crushing chest pain or syncope
Dismissing the symptoms as 'just anxiety' or reflux without excluding a cardiac cause
errors_of_commission: Confirming 'benign mitral valve prolapse — your heart is safe' over chat without the auscultation/echo the diagnosis rests on (false reassurance)
Prescribing a beta-blocker or an anxiolytic remotely without assessment
Over-triaging to an emergency ambulance (000) for stable, brief, non-exertional low-risk symptoms with no red flags
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass REQUIRES arranging objective in-person cardiac assessment AND delivering safety-netting. False reassurance over chat, remote prescribing, or dismissing the presentation as anxiety is a fail regardless of other content.
